issue_title: Decimals cause an error
PyPI releases!
issue_body:
At `PyAstro16` we discussed that this `astropy/regions` package should be developed quickly in this repo at https://github.com/astropy/regions/ and then be merged in the Astropy core repo.

There hasn't been much development on `astropy/regions` after `PyAstro16` though, there isn't much functionality, tests, docs yet ... it's not clear if it's feasible to get this done and stable before for Astropy 1.3.

I'd like to propose that we start doing releases on PyPI and announce them on `astropy-dev`, starting with 0.1 very soon (in a week or two).

A personal motivation for that is that I don't like having to update the bundled version of this package at [gammapy.extern.regions](https://github.com/gammapy/gammapy/tree/master/gammapy/extern/regions) because we have started using it for circles and need it to be available to end users. The main reason is that I think releases on PyPI and announcements on astropy-dev might attract some co-developers to this package and having had users means that the API and implementation is likely more robust when the time comes to move this to Astropy core.

I'd be happy to make the releases, and if someone else (@joleroi, @keflavich) wants to, even better!

Concerning package name, I propose plain `regions` (which amazing wasn't taken, so I reserved it right now: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/regions ) This is nice because it's the same as the current repo and package name, i.e. no confusion by users or developers.

I did consider `astropy-regions` as package name. That's more explicit, but also longer and then some people get confused if PyPI package and installed package names are different.

@keflavich @astrofrog @joleroi @eteq @larrybradley - Thoughts?

issue_title: arXiv: allow verbose post 2007 strings
issue_body:
In inspirehep we have some arXiv numbers tokenized as:
```
arXiv:yymm.xxxx [hep-th]
```

The current implementation does not allow for any trailing strings after the number even if in the arXiv documentation it's said that:

> Note that the identifier no longer contains any classification information and thus reclassification after an article is announced is possible. Classification information is indicated in other ways. For example, the stamp added to the side of the PDF versions of arXiv articles has the form:
>
> arXiv:0706.0001v1 [q-bio.CB] 1 Jun 2007

`arXiv:0706.0001v1 [q-bio.CB] 1 Jun 2007` is not exactly an arXiv number, but, it might be useful for idutils to accept such strings as arXiv numbers.

issue_title: RFC celery: msgpack could support custom types
issue_body:
### Intro
In Invenio we use `Celery`, which uses `kombu` to serialize messages. `kombu` supports a number of backends, of which we use `msgpack`.

### Issue
`msgpack` supports many native types (strings, lists, etc), but has no support for more complex types such as `datetime` and `enum`, or, naturally, any custom types we define in our code. This is an issue because business logic should contain serialization code.

### Solution
Fortunately `msgpack` us to extend its serialization functionality, by providing 128 "slots" for application-specific types. In fact, not only does this allow us to add support

### Proposal
With a bit of glue [(WIP PoC PR)](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/pull/3294) and perhaps the assistance of `flask-registry`, modules would be able to define type matchers as well as serializers and deserializers for the types that they will want to use, even if they are specific to themselves.

cc. @jalavik @jirikuncar @hachreak

issue_title: Add support for PATCH method
Encryption of user password in request
issue_body:
Maybe I've missed something, but shouldn't there be a possibility to encrypt or hash the password on the client side before sending it to the REST endpoint? When you don't have a TLS encrypted connection this could add some security.
Of course you would then have to compare the encrypted password directly with the one from the django database.

issue_title: Failure to build apk due to incorrect invocation of "ant" by "sh.ant"...
issue_body:
[This issue report represents a condensation of the exchange with Alexander Taylor on the google group for p4a.  If interested, please reference original at https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/python-android/2fLGqp6Bcec  .]


I'm using the new (master) branch of python-for-android (at commit 93728aa), on top of a clean, up-to-date install of Debian jessie; all package dependencies are installed as listed in the p4a readme docs. I'm finding the error shown below.

I see the same error (below) whether I call p4a directly, or if I use buildozer to call it (via "android_new").  The old-toolchain of python-for-android works fine on this system to produce APKs.  The error below seems strange to me, as the command being called by p4a:

  /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant debug

doesn't seem right to me (more below).

Here's my call to p4a:

  p4a apk --sdk_dir /opt/android-sdk-linux --ndk_dir /opt/android-ndk-r10e \
          --android_api 15 --ndk_ver r10e-rc4 --private /root/HelloWorld \
          --package=org.hw.helloworld --name="HelloWorld" \
          --version=0.5 --bootstrap=pygame --requirements=sdl,python2 \
							  		  --dist_name=helloworld

Here's some initial logging output from p4a showing things going well:

  [INFO]:    This python-for-android revamp is an experimental alpha release!
  [INFO]:    It should work (mostly), but you may experience missing features or bugs.
  [INFO]:    Will compile for the following archs: armeabi
  [INFO]:    Getting Android API version from user argument
  [INFO]:    Available Android APIs are (15)
  [INFO]:    Requested API target 15 is available, continuing.
  [INFO]:    Getting NDK dir from from user argument
  [INFO]:    Got NDK version from from user argument
  [INFO]:    Using Google NDK r10e-rc4
  [INFO]:    Found virtualenv at /usr/local/bin/virtualenv
  [INFO]:    Found the following toolchain versions: ['4.8', '4.9', 'clang3.5', 'clang3.6']
  [INFO]:    Picking the latest gcc toolchain, here 4.9
  [INFO]:    Of the existing distributions, the following meet the given requirements:
  [INFO]:       helloworld: includes recipes (hostpython2, pygame_bootstrap_components, python2, sdl, six, pygame, pyjnius, android, kivy), built for archs (armeabi)
  [INFO]:    helloworld has compatible recipes, using this one
  [INFO]:    Of the existing distributions, the following meet the given requirements:
  [INFO]:       helloworld: includes recipes (hostpython2, pygame_bootstrap_components, python2, sdl, six, pygame, pyjnius, android, kivy), built for archs (armeabi)
  [INFO]:    helloworld has compatible recipes, using this one
  [INFO]:    -> directory context /root/.local/share/python-for-android/dists/helloworld
  <snip>

Here's the relevant snippet of log output showing the error:

  [INFO]:    -> running ant debug
  [INFO]:    STDOUT:
       Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "/usr/local/bin/p4a", line 9, in <module>
      load_entry_point('python-for-android==0.3', 'console_scripts', 'p4a')()
    File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pythonforandroid/toolchain.py", line 708, in main
      ToolchainCL()
    File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pythonforandroid/toolchain.py", line 323, in __init__
      getattr(self, args.command)(unknown)
    File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pythonforandroid/toolchain.py", line 106, in wrapper_func
      func(self, args)
    File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pythonforandroid/toolchain.py", line 556, in apk
      shprint(sh.ant, 'debug', _tail=20, _critical=True)
    File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pythonforandroid/logger.py", line 164, in shprint
      output = command(*args, **kwargs)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/sh.py", line 769, in __call__
      return RunningCommand(cmd, call_args, stdin, stdout, stderr)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/sh.py", line 327, in __init__
      self.call_args, pipe=pipe)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/sh.py", line 891, in __init__
      os.execv(cmd[0], cmd)
  OSError: [Errno 13] Permission denied
  [INFO]:    STDERR:
  [INFO]:    COMMAND:
    cd /root/.local/share/python-for-android/dists/helloworld && /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant debug

  [WARNING]: ERROR: /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant failed!


But, the command being executed ("/opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant") is referencing what is the ant *DIRECTORY*, not any ant executable (so the permission denied error message is unsurprising).  At least that's the internal structure of the Android SDK (r24.4.1) I downloaded and extracted earlier today:

  root@laptop:~# ls -d /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant/*
  /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant/build.xml
  /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant/NOTICE
  /opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant/uibuild.xml

Python-for-android is definitely trying to call the ant executable above (using its "sh.ant" functionality) --- what strikes me as strange, though, are two aspects of this call:

 (1) the call to ant uses a fully-canonicalized path (shown above, in log from my run as: "/opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant").  So, irrespective of my particular $PATH setting, or location of ant on my system, it's not going to search any further locations than that one.  Incidentally, though not mentioned above (I should have), my version of ant was installed via standard Debian (Jessie) package, so the primary ant executable lives at normal location of "/usr/bin/ant".  (If the solution were as simple as more careful construction of my $PATH env variable, I probably wouldn't have even posted here.)

 (2) the fully-canonicalized path that python-for-android calls ("/opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant") just happens to correspond to that of a directory for ant in the android SDK as extracted (r24.4.1), so there is indeed an "ant" object at that location, it's just that it's an ant directory, not the expected ant binary executable.  Weird to see this coincidence (?), and not sure what to make of it.

In examining why "sh.ant" canonicalizes ant to "/opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant" as above, let's consider the output on my system from a few key commands:

  root@laptop:~# whereis ant
  ant: /usr/bin/ant /usr/share/ant /usr/share/man/man1/ant.1.gz
  root@laptop:~# which ant
  /usr/bin/ant
  root@laptop:~# ls -l /usr/bin/ant /usr/share/ant
  lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root   20 Oct  7  2014 /usr/bin/ant -> ../share/ant/bin/ant
  
  /usr/share/ant:
  total 12
  drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Mar  7 21:02 bin
  drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 Mar  7 21:02 etc
  drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Mar  7 21:02 lib
  root@laptop:~# ls -l /usr/bin/ant
  lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 20 Oct  7  2014 /usr/bin/ant -> ../share/ant/bin/ant
  root@laptop:~# whereis -l
  bin: /usr/bin
  bin: /usr/sbin
  bin: /usr/lib
  bin: /bin
  bin: /sbin
  bin: /etc
  bin: /lib
  bin: /lib64
  bin: /usr/games
  bin: /usr/local/bin

  bin: /usr/local/etc
  bin: /usr/local/lib
  bin: /usr/local/games
  bin: /usr/include
  bin: /usr/local
  bin: /usr/share
  man: /usr/share/man/fr
  man: /usr/share/man/man7
  man: /usr/share/man/es
  man: /usr/share/man/sr

  man: /usr/share/man/cs

  man: /usr/share/man/gl
  man: /usr/share/man/man2
  man: /usr/share/man/man5
  man: /usr/share/man/da
  man: /usr/share/man/man6
  man: /usr/share/man/ar
  man: /usr/share/man/man3
  man: /usr/share/man/uk
  man: /usr/share/man/ug
  man: /usr/share/man/id
  man: /usr/share/man/hu
  man: /usr/share/man/sk
  man: /usr/share/man/zh_TW
  man: /usr/share/man/ru
  man: /usr/share/man/ko
  man: /usr/share/man/el
  man: /usr/share/man/de
  man: /usr/share/man/man4
  man: /usr/share/man/ja
  man: /usr/share/man/pt
  man: /usr/share/man/sv
  man: /usr/share/man/fi
  man: /usr/share/man/tr
  man: /usr/share/man/zh_CN
  man: /usr/share/man/nl
  man: /usr/share/man/it
  man: /usr/share/man/ca
  man: /usr/share/man/man8
  man: /usr/share/man/man1
  man: /usr/share/man/pt_BR
  root@laptop:~#


It's interesting to note that "whereis ant" does not actually find the "/opt/android-sdk-linux/tools/ant" command invocation, as identified by "sh.ant" in p4a.  Instead, it correctly identifies "/usr/bin/ant" (among others).  Further, it's apparent that whereis does not even *look* in the "/opt" directory (see "whereis -l" above), so "sh.ant" must be getting that from somewhere else.  One possibility to consider is that you'll note (from my previous message here) that I use the "--sdk" command parameter to pass the location of the Android SDK to p4a, rather than using an environmental variable; according to p4a docs, that's fully supported, though.

Incidentally, I've tried this as both root, and as a normal user, just to see if it mattered (it did not, as I'd suspected).

Thanks for your thoughts on a bug fix.

issue_title: evaluate() generates an extra reference for objects in calling frame
issue_body:
I find that `numexpr.evaluate()` generates an extra ref count for objects in calling frame, even when the object isn't used in the expression.  Here's an example:

```python
import numexpr
import numpy as np
import sys

def f():
    a = np.arange(10)
    numexpr.evaluate('1')
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))

def g():
    a = np.arange(10)
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))

f()
g()
```
```
3
2
```

This created a memory leak in my code: in my application, `a` was a big array that I was deleting, but it was never garbage collected because of the reference created by numexpr in a totally unrelated operation.

Notably, passing `local_dict={}` fixes the problem.  I think the usage of `sys._getframe().f_locals` is creating the persistent reference somehow, perhaps via a caching mechanism.  So this code also exhibits the leak:

```python
def h():
    a = np.arange(10)
    sys._getframe(0).f_locals
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))
h()
```

If I had to guess, I'd say this has to do with CPython fast locals (https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/master/Objects/frameobject.c), but I'm not enough of an expert to be able to tell for certain.

If one captures the f_locals dict then calls `clear()` on it, the leak disappears.  I have no idea if this is safe, though!

issue_title: Interaction and Correlation Features
issue_body:
This post describes a possible enhancement, but I'm not sure it fits in with the alphalens project objective as it deals with factor combination. I am proposing it because alphalens already has some of the functions already in place to make this type of analysis easy. However, I realize it may be better included in a separate project.

When combining alpha factors, analysts often look at the correlation between factors to determine whether we are adding anything "new" to the signal or if the two factors simply measure the same thing.
(Ideally we would want factors with low correlation).

Additionally, it is interesting to know whether two factors might have an interaction effect where instead of the effects combining in an additive manner, they do so in a multiplicative manner.

References on Interaction Effects:
https://courses.washington.edu/smartpsy/interactions.htm
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interaction_(statistics

Below, I have included a link to a Jupyter notebook that uses the size and momentum factors from the [Ken French data library](http://mba.tuck.dartmouth.edu/pages/faculty/ken.french/data_library.html) as an example of what the output might look like. (The implementation in alphalens if included would be different of course). https://www.dropbox.com/s/owh8rfumuch4c9g/Momentum%20and%20Size%20Interaction-2.ipynb?dl=0

Here is a snippet of the output from the notebook:
![heatmap](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28760724/32498468-cbe12d62-c39d-11e7-80e7-abeb63002d15.PNG)
![interaction plot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28760724/32498473-cfff63dc-c39d-11e7-9896-46321103d12a.PNG)
![correlation plot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28760724/32498481-d480fbaa-c39d-11e7-9545-f55c3b378719.PNG)

As mentioned above, I'm not 100% sure this fits in with the alphalens project, as alphalens tends to focus on the analysis of a single alpha factor. However, the reason I bring it up is that alphalens already has some of the functions that make this type of factor combination analysis easy. Essentially, the factor_data dataframe (returned from `utils.get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns()`) contains the data needed for the interaction analysis (forward returns, factor values and quantiles).  

Any feedback or questions are appreciated.

issue_title: Spaces in path
issue_body:
I am having trouble building GalSim when I am working in a directory that includes a space in the parent. I haven't quite tracked down whether it's Scons or GalSim that is causing the problem. This is with `scons DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH="$LSST_LIBRARY_PATH"` (ie I have quoted the path in the scons call and GalSim reports the correct value and quotes it properly later on:

Lines in `config.log` give a clue:
```
bash -c "DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH='/Volumes/G-RAID with Thunderbolt/transient/lsstsw/src/galsim/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/tmv/0.73+a0e9a6df0f/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/fftw/3.3.4.lsst2+da39a3ee5e/lib:/Volumes/G-RAID with Thunderbolt/transient/lsstsw/src/qserv/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/xrootd/4.3.0a.lsst1+da39a3ee5e/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/sphgeom/12.0.rc1+6bbdc56f4c/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/protobuf/2.6.1.lsst3+fbf04ba888/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/partition/12.0.rc1+144e0d2e07/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/libevent/2.0.16.lsst2+da39a3ee5e/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/mariadb/10.1.11.lsst2+da39a3ee5e/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/mariadbclient/10.1.11.lsst2+da39a3ee5e/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/base/12.0.rc1+0a04801fb9/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/apr_util/1.5.4+f7ed051de0/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/apr/1.5.2+da39a3ee5e/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/log4cxx/0.10.0.lsst6+1cc8a4a234/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/log/12.0.rc1+8b859c332d/lib:/Users/timj/work/lsstsw/stack/DarwinX86/boost/1.60+fbf04ba888/lib' /Volumes/G-RAID with Thunderbolt/transient/lsstsw/src/galsim/.sconf_temp/conftest_11" > .sconf_temp/conftest_11.out
bash: /Volumes/G-RAID: No such file or directory
```

issue_title: dynet parameter subset update error
issue_body:
Take this as an example, it works fine. 
```
import dynet as dy
import numpy as np

model = dy.Model()
pW = model.add_parameters((2, 4))
pb = model.add_parameters(2)
trainer = dy.SimpleSGDTrainer(model)

def step(update_b):

    x = dy.inputTensor(np.ones(4))
    W = pW.expr()
    # update b?
    b = pb.expr(update = update_b)

    loss = dy.pickneglogsoftmax(W * x + b, 0)
    loss.backward()
    trainer.update()
    dy.renew_cg()

print(pb.as_array())
print(pW.as_array())
step(True)
print(pb.as_array()) # b updated
print(pW.as_array())
step(False)
print(pb.as_array()) # b not updated
print(pW.as_array())
```
If you just replace the order of step(False), and step(True) it gives an error: 

>  loss = dy.pickneglogsoftmax(W * x + b, 0)
>   File "_dynet.pyx", line 1899, in _dynet.Expression.__add__
> NotImplementedErro

This could be avoided by moving `dy.renew_cg()` to the beginning of step function. But, still I believe this is a bug.

issue_title: Retire matInput from python?
issue_body:
Following questions on #345 , and now that we have inputTensor, perhaps we should consider retiring matInput (replacing it with a blank op that throws an error and asks the user to switch to inputTensor).

We really don't need both functionalities, and the way inputTensor handles things is cleaner.
The difference in functionality I do see is that matInput supports setting a new value (for "static" graphs). We can either add this to inputTensor, or remove the functionality altogether.

issue_title: RTD uses a cached virtualenv, which makes subpackage content stale
issue_body:
(I [recently noticed](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pull/2571#issuecomment-255297482) this, though it's been happening since we switched to subpackages.) For example, in a [recent build](https://readthedocs.org/projects/google-cloud-python/builds/4542871/):

```
Processing ./core
  Requirement already satisfied (use --upgrade to upgrade):
  google-cloud-core==0.20.0 from file:///.../checkouts/latest/core in
  /home/.../envs/latest/lib/python2.7/site-packages
  (from -r docs/requirements.txt (line 1))
Processing ./bigquery
  Requirement already satisfied (use --upgrade to upgrade):
  google-cloud-bigquery==0.20.0 from file:///.../checkouts/latest/bigquery in
  /home/.../envs/latest/lib/python2.7/site-packages
  (from -r docs/requirements.txt (line 2))
```

If we were building these on a machine we owned, it would be no big deal, we'd force the packages to reinstall (`--ignore-installed`) or we'd just create a brand new virtual environment. However, on [RTD](https://www.readthedocs.org) the only toggles we have are `docs/requirements.txt`, `docs/conf.py` and `setup.py` (at the root).

I spent about 30m messing with the `requirements.txt` trying to [convince it to accept the flag](https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/17), and trying to see if there was any other hack (by specifying a `git` URL instead of a filesystem path). The "recommeneded" RTD fix is to install as editable, but `pip` and `setuptools` [are b0rken](https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3) with editable installs in namespace packages. Yay!

/cc @jonparrott

issue_title: enable pool to have unique id (name) and to be easily deleted from saved pools.
issue_body: I hold onto the pool and reuse it for any new requests of a pool -- in theory, that's faster. However, I should let a new pool be created, and the old one be destroyed. I should probably add a new method (destroy?) to delete the old pool, and maybe also a keyword for Pool.__init__, that would force deletion of the old instance and replace it with a new instance.

issue_title: Add support for healthCheckGracePeriodSeconds for ECS services.
issue_body:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/service_definition_parameters.html

healthCheckGracePeriodSeconds
The period of time, in seconds, that the Amazon ECS service scheduler should ignore unhealthy Elastic Load Balancing target health checks after a task has first started. This is only valid if your service is configured to use a load balancer. If your service's tasks take a while to start and respond to health checks, you can specify a health check grace period of up to 1,800 seconds during which the ECS service scheduler ignores the health check status. This grace period can prevent the ECS service scheduler from marking tasks as unhealthy and stopping them before they have time to come up.

issue_title: Wrong prop name in rds.OptionGroup()
issue_body:
The `OptionGroupConfigurations` prop should be named `OptionConfigurations` according to [AWS docs] (http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-rds-optiongroup.html).

Otherwise template creation will fail with:
> Encountered unsupported property OptionGroupConfigurations

issue_title: creating addresses from changed files fails in a confusing way if the changed filenames contain spaces
issue_body: If a changed file has a space, then pants will ignore the part of the path for that file that comes before the space. This can show up as a `glob outside the build root` error if the space is before a path separator, eg `a/b /c`, and can be frustrating to debug.

issue_title: source in more than one target: goal idea silently excludes lots of files
issue_body:
over in Twitter-land...
A fun Twitter project had code that could be summarized as...

tests/scala/BUILD
  sources=rglobs('*.scala')

tests/scala/sub/BUILD
  sources=globs('*scala')

./pants goal idea src/:: reasonably had problems; you expect problems if a source is in more than one target.

Ideally, pants would have complained loudly.

Instead, it created a project that excluded many source files from IntelliJ's file view. D'oh, confusing to the user.

issue_title: album compalition
issue_body:
Hello, 
i had an error in the data_structures.py file:
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/soco/data_structures.py", line 48, in get_didl_object
    cls = DIDL_CLASS_TO_CLASS[xml.find(ns_tag('upnp', 'class')).text]
KeyError: 'object.container.album.musicAlbum.compilation'

I think it's because the data structure for album compilitons aren't defined. I think it's because I set the album grouping in the sonos settings to "itunes compalitions",

![bildschirmfoto 2015-01-21 um 19 50 56](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1587123/5842668/f53c381c-a1a6-11e4-945b-421f5f73cb53.png)
 
I solved it by adding an DidlMusicAlbumCompilation class after the DidlMusicAlbum class definition. It is the same as the DidlMusicAlbum but with an item_class = 'object.container.album.musicAlbum.compilation'

Perhaps you can add this to the next version of soco.

I don't know if there is an easier way to do so. 

My new class declaration:

class DidlMusicAlbumCompilation(DidlAlbum):

    """Class that represents a music library compalition album.

    :ivar item_class: The item_class for DidlMusicTrack is
        'object.container.album.musicAlbum.compilation'
    :ivar _translation: The dictionary-key-to-xml-tag-and-namespace-
        translation used when instantiating a DidlAlbum from XML. The value is
        shown below

        .. code-block :: python

            # key: (ns, tag)
            _translation = {
                'title': ('dc', 'title'),
                'creator': ('dc', 'creator'),
                'album_art_uri': ('upnp', 'albumArtURI'),
                'uri': ('', 'res')
            }

    """

    item_class = 'object.container.album.musicAlbum.compilation'
    # name: (ns, tag)
    _translation = {
        'title': ('dc', 'title'),
        'creator': ('dc', 'creator'),
        'album_art_uri': ('upnp', 'albumArtURI'),
        'uri': ('', 'res')
    }

    @property
    def album_art_uri(self):
        """Get and set the album art URI as an unicode object."""
        return self.content.get('album_art_uri')

    @album_art_uri.setter
    def album_art_uri(self, album_art_uri):  # pylint: disable=C0111
        self.content['album_art_uri'] = album_art_uri

issue_title: Get sonos favourites
issue_body:
I like this project especially because it allows you to use almost every feature of Sonos.
Recently I realized that accessing the sonos favourites is not yet possible with SoCo and found [this](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/python-soco/TILyrsXghbg) workaround on Google Groups.

Integrating this functionality into SoCo shouldn't be a big deal:
- In the core.py, the SEARCH_TRANSLATION dict should be extended by the pair
  'sonos_favourites': 'FV:2'
- get_sonos_favourites() should be added as a convenience method for
  get_music_library_information('sonos_favourites')
- this change should be documented in the wiki

Is there some issue I am missing? Otherwise, I will start working on this in a fork.

issue_title: Hide download button in Android app WebView
issue_body: In the Android app, downloading files from the content page isn't currently supported, as webviews don't have built-in filepicker functionality. There are ways we could add that eventually, but it's also not a critical function for the standalone Android app, so at least for now, just hiding the button when we detect it's inside a webview would be best.

issue_title: Allow URI input form on replay to work without JS
issue_body:
Per #412, we added a button to submit the form, which previously relied on hitting enter with a text field in focus. The conventional submission mechanism is currently suppressed via JS and handled via JS.

This should be more accessible. @ibnesayeed provided a mechanism to accomplish this in #412.

issue_title: Python3 BatchJobHelper AttributeError
issue_body:
This issue was initially reported [here](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/adwords-api/39kYsLsV3Ys).

There is issue with the BatchJobService in Python 3 where an AttributeError is raised in the **_GetRawOperationsFromXML** method in this line:

```      root = ElementTree.fromstring(raw_request_xml.encode('utf-8'))```

This will work in Python 2 because `str` has an encode method, whereas Python 3's `bytes` does not.

We'll try to sneak this in to an upcoming release and close this issue when it is out.

issue_title: Args keys not in parameter error when parameter is xml code
issue_body:
Hi!

I try to get data from a SharePoint list.

I want to call GetListItems from a SharePoint list.
The simplest request should look like this:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soap12env:Envelope xmlns:sharepoint="http://schemas.microsoft.com/sharepoint/soap/"
                    xmlns:soap12env="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope">
  <soap12env:Header/>
  <soap12env:Body>
    <sharepoint:GetListItems>
      <sharepoint:listName>MyList</sharepoint:listName>
      <sharepoint:viewName/>
      <sharepoint:query/>
      <sharepoint:viewFields/>
      <sharepoint:rowLimit/>
      <sharepoint:queryOptions><QueryOptions /></sharepoint:queryOptions>
      <sharepoint:webID/>
    </sharepoint:GetListItems>
  </soap12env:Body>
</soap12env:Envelope>
```
I would generate this with the following PySimpleSoap calls:

```python
    client = SoapClient(wsdl='https://my.sharepoint.site.com/_vti_bin/lists.asmx?wsdl',
                        soap_ns="soap12env", ns="sharepoint",
                        trace=True)
    response = client.GetListItems(listName='MyList',
                                   viewName='',
                                   query='',
                                   viewFields='',
                                   rowLimit='',
                                   queryOptions='<QueryOptions />',
                                   webID=''
                                   )
```

But I get the following error:
```
Failed: Invalid Args Structure. Errors: [u'Args keys not in parameter. parameter: {}, args: <QueryOptions />']
```

How can I pass xml formated parameter values like <QueryOptions /> to a function call?

issue_title: swarm services: extra_hosts set in wrong format
issue_body:
Dear all,
we encounter issues with setting `extra_hosts` for swarm services on docker-py 2.6.1

```
docker version
Client:
 Version:      17.06.2-ce
 API version:  1.30
 Go version:   go1.8.3
 Git commit:   cec0b72
 Built:        Tue Sep  5 19:59:06 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:
 Version:      17.06.2-ce
 API version:  1.30 (minimum version 1.12)
 Go version:   go1.8.3
 Git commit:   cec0b72
 Built:        Tue Sep  5 20:00:25 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
 Experimental: false
```
**What we do**
```
extra_hosts = dict()
extra_hosts.update({'domain.com': '8.8.8.8'})
client.services.create(name='myservice', image='nginx', hosts=extra_hosts)
```

**Result**
The entry is not found in `/etc/hosts` in the container.
The attribute `Hosts` of the service's `ContainerSpec` shows the following syntax: `domain.com:8.8.8.8`
However, the syntax should have been `8.8.8.8 domain.com`

**Analysis**
docker-py uses this line to format `extra_hosts` (https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/2.6.1/docker/utils/utils.py#L569):
```
 '{}:{}'.format(k, v) for k, v in sorted(six.iteritems(extra_hosts))
```

According to swarm's API (https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/d91c5f42eb37c6f88cec4021c10c0a1ded1785c3/api/types/swarm/container.go#L65), the ContainerSpec of a TaskSpec expects `extra_hosts` in the format described per http://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/hosts.5.html which is:
```
IP HostnameA HostnameB ...
```

**Workaround**
Currently, we compensate this as follows:
```
create_service_kwargs = docker.models.services._get_create_service_kwargs(...)
create_service_kwargs['task_template']['ContainerSpec']['Hosts'] = ['8.8.8.8 domain.com']
client.api.create_service(**create_service_kwargs)
```

Maybee you can comment on this.

Thanks and Regards,
Stephen

issue_title: Conflict between 'docker' and 'docker-py' packages on PyPI.
issue_body:
Based on my relatively quick analysis it would appear that there are 2 packages for this project on PyPI: one for v1.x and one for v2.x. At some point in the middle - perhaps in v2.0 - the name of the package was renamed from 'docker-py' to 'docker'. Typically this would not cause a problem since the two packages have unique names and thus should be able to be installed side-by-side, however from what I can tell v1 of the project installs to the same target folder as v2 (ie: site-packages/docker) which can cause all sorts of packaging conflicts. For a trivial example, simply try the following:

> pip install docker
> python -c 'from docker import APIClient'      # Operation succeeds
> pip install docker-py
> python -c 'from docker import APIClient'      # Operation fails

I hit this problem recently while working on a new Python project which uses the newer 'docker' package, but I attempted to install another 3rd party lib which transitively depends on 'docker-py'. Install it over top of my current runtime environment essentially corrupted my environment. Said project is already being updated to work with 'docker' but the underlying compatibility problems between 'docker' and 'docker-py' really should be resolved to avoid problems like this in other packages.

issue_title: Counter input : read methods on NIdaqmx version < 10
issue_body:
Hello,

Trying to use a `counter input` I noticed few things:

(1) the `stream_readers.CounterReader` Class can be wrongly used : for instance, by calling `read_many_sample_pulse_time` for a counter that does not measure pulse time. The help in readthedocs could be clearer about this ("use the method X only if the counter is X"), and the error messages as well. 

(2) for NIdaqmx version < 10 (like in my case) - and maybe other version of Nidaqmx - the functions `_read_counter_f_64_ex` and `_read_counter_u_32_ex` are NOT available. So the call to `read()` or to `CounterReader` should call `_read_counter_f_64` and `_read_counter_u_32` instead (by probing the driver version with 

> system = nidaqmx.system.System.local()
DriverVersion=system.driver_version
major_version = DriverVersion[0] # 9 in my case

eventually).
The function should also raise a `warning` to update the driver, optionally.


NIdaqmx version 9.2.3
Python 64 bits on Windows 7 x64

issue_title: _convert_timestamp uses timezone information where it should not
issue_body:
When _convert_timestamp gets a datetime object with timezone data, it converts it to the system's default via timegm. Also when given a datetime object without timezone data, it assumes it to be the system's local timezone.

From this data, the timestamp is created. So the timestamp is always in local timezone.

This is wrong as influxdb requires UTC timestamps. This is especially important in my timezone (Europe/Berlin, currently +02:00) where current timestamps are inserted in influxdb's future and are not shown in queries.

The fix is to create timestamps from a datetime object that is converted to UTC first.

Our current workaround is to convert timestamps before calling line_protocol.make_lines like this:

time = int(mydatetimeobject.astimezone(pytz.UTC).replace(tzinfo=None).timestamp() * 1e9)

issue_title: feat(router): Add HTTP to HTTPs redirect
issue_body:
This was attempted in #2635 and again in #2782, but the implementation in those PRs doesn't seem to work. This is a desired feature, so I wanted to track it separately so it wasn't lost.

I've attempted this myself, but it appears the `server` blocks keep conflicting in the nginx template. After several failed attempts at this, I resorted to [asking StackOverflow](http://stackoverflow.com/a/27711250/644992) for help. An answer seems worthy of attempting, but I haven't had the time to get back to this, and likely will be working on other things for the next Deis release.

I wanted to document this so hopefully someone else can pick it up.

issue_title: Add auth:whoami endpoint
issue_body:
Hi there,

TL;DR It would be great to add an endpoint for the `whoami` command (or add the new one) and make it return all user's informations from Deis.

Example Request:

```
GET /v1/whoami HTTP/1.1
Host: deis.example.com
Authorization: token abc123
```

Example Response:

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
DEIS_API_VERSION: 1.7
DEIS_PLATFORM_VERSION: 1.12.2
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "id": 1,
  "last_login": "2014-10-19T22:01:00.601Z",
  "is_superuser": true,
  "username": "test",
  "first_name": "test",
  "last_name": "testerson",
  "email": "test@example.com",
  "is_staff": true,
  "is_active": true,
  "date_joined": "2014-10-19T22:01:00.601Z",
  "groups": [],
  "user_permissions": []
}
```

## Background

We have a platform with multiple, independent Deis clusters. There is an application with API that synchronizes all informations and a plugin for Deis client. It allows developers to manage databases and their backups through that plugin and application.

## The problem

We would like to allow to destroy database only for application's owner and deny it to the contributors. We are using token to authenticate user against Deis cluster and check his permissions, but we are not able to recognize if he is an owner or not.

We don't want to use informations from the local profile file, because it may be manipulated by the user (you can change your local username by editing this file).


When syncing, sometimes a peer will send unexpected "parts".  Currently, those are dumped to the terminal.  This has two primary problems.

- causes a **lot** of noise.
- the algorithm for detecting *unexpected* parts doesn't take into account parts that have already been received.

### How can it be fixed

- split into two sets, duplicates, and parts we didn't ask for (unexpected)
- log at debug level.

issue_title: Middleware to sign transactions locally
issue_body:
* Version: 4

## What was wrong?

It would be convenient to use the `Web3.eth` interface as if you were using your node's account management features, even if the key is only available locally.

Basically, we want this to work:

```
from web3.auto import w3

# some middleware setup on w3, with private key 0x000...0001

w3.eth.sendTransaction({ ... normal transaction ... , 'from': '0xAccountForPrivateKey1'})
```

## How can it be fixed?

**New Local Middleware**

A reasonable approach is to add a new middleware that is configured with a private key. It would look for transactions from the account with the associated private key, sign it locally, and send it to the node already signed.

issue_title: Warn that eth-account has not been audited
issue_body:
* Version: 4.0.0


## What was wrong?

`eth-account` is pending audit, but there is an expectation that `w3.eth.account` will be included with web3.py v4. We don't want people who see that v4 is going stable to mistake that as a signal that `eth-account` is ready for prod use.

## How can it be fixed?

### Do all of these:

- Include a big warning box at the top of http://web3py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/web3.eth.account.html saying something like: "This has not gone through security review"
- Some kind of explicit opt-in process
- Add a section to the docs explaining how to enable `w3.eth.account`

### Alternatives for opt-in process:

#### Approaches

1. Install "extra" to opt-in
2. Flip runtime flag to opt in
3. ??

#### Approach Detail

1. Don't install `eth-account` unless user installs web3 with `pip install web3[unaudited-private-keys]`. At startup, if `eth_account` is not available, create a `w3.eth.account` property that immediately throws on access, explaining:
    a. what the property is
    b. ask if they meant `w3.eth.accounts`
    c. why it's not enabled by default
    d. how to enable it
2. Always install `eth-account`, but immediately install the throwing `w3.eth.account` property  (see: a, b, c, d from 1). Add a `w3.eth.i_know_private_key_code_is_unaudited = True` property setter that installs `eth-account` to `w3.eth.account`.

#### Tradeoffs

1. Install "extra" to opt-in
   - Benefit: easy to get started; one-time opt-in
   - Downside: if eth-account was already installed (eg~ because they were on the beta), then they can "accidentally" opt in without noticing
2. Flip runtime flag to opt in
   - Benefit: No way to "accidentally" opt in, error message offers a straightforward solution
   - Downside: Have to flip the flag on every run, annoying at terminal

#### Current favorite

Between these, I'm leaning to 2. We can get rid off all this nonsense after the audit. In the meantime, I'd rather have it be a bit more annoying than to have people accidentally opt-in.

In the short term, for people at the terminal, we could guide them to use `from eth_account import Account` directly.

#### Something else?

@pipermerriam any other ideas or thoughts on these?

issue_title: EthCC 2018 Paris Workshop Results
issue_body:
My apologies to the workshop group. You unintentionally became documentation testers. The good news is that your struggles were not in vain! I marked down what I could about where people got stuck, and we'll see what we can do about improving the situation.

### What was wrong?

1. Old version of pip, setuptools
2. Run virtualenv setup in python console
3. Virtualenv not installed
4. Using python 2, ended up with web3 v3, because there is no v4 version available
5. Using web3 v3 (missed "--pre" during install)
6. Connection is very confusing for people: why do they have to connect to anything? how do they specify what to connect to? what are all these different providers for and why are we making them pick one?
7. Tried to use web3.auto for infura
8. Can't deploy via infura
9. Faucet down
10. Ethtoken example only works on mainnet
11. Ethtoken docs did not show how to customize w3
12. People not clear when w3 is calling the node and when working locally
13. Didn't know how to fix address checksum error
14. Pip wasn't installed
15. Could not understand solc missing error (installed separate from pysolc)


### How can it be fixed?


1. Add pip & setuptools update to quickstart instructions
2. Clearer distinction between shell and python interpreter
3. Add (link to) instructions for installing `virtualenv`
4. maybe post a v4 version built for py2 exclusively for the purpose of installing and warning that py3 is required?
5. Wait for v4 release
6. ???
7. Maybe we should fall back to infura if we haven't found any other connection?
8. Wait for #666 
9. In docs, link to a list of several faucets for different testnets. (There's probably already a good ESE post with this
10. Write a working ethtoken example for testnet
1. Add ethtoken docs for customizing w3 in ethtoken for testnet
2. In v5, consider a separate web3utils module that is clearly never hitting the network (can host things like: type conversions, sha3, eth-account)
3. #716 
4. Add (link to) pip install instructions for a variety of platforms
5. https://github.com/ethereum/py-solc/issues/40

issue_title: proxy authentication not working
issue_body:
I've been having an issue with conda authenticating with our companies proxy server.  If I set the HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY environment variables with the username and password it works fine, but saving my password in plain sight isn't a viable option.  The conda configuration states:

"Note: If you do not include the username and password, of if authentication fails, conda will prompt for a username and password."

So I tried setting the proxies without the username and password in my .condarc, but get the following problem:

.condarc
```
# Proxy settings: http://[username]:[password]@[server]:[port]
proxy_servers:
    http: http://proxy.company.com:8080
    https: https://proxy.company.com:8080
ssl_verify: true
```
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9438047/17027116/dd9579d0-4f1f-11e6-85a0-e0a688f71182.png)

First, my password is still in plain sight, and second, there seems to be a bug since it prints `https proxy username:` again after I've typed the username but before the `Password:` prompt. Once I hit enter after typing my password, it prompts `https proxy username:` again and as soon as I touch any key it prints the `Password:` prompt.

A fix or any help would be much appreciated.

Thanks.

issue_title: [API Users] Can't run set_role with no value
issue_body:
You can't run the CLI command: 'username test no role', which is what is used when you 
invoke: node.api('users').set_role('test')

As an aside, this missed testing because the function names weren't prefixed with test_ so python's unittest never ran them.

The faulty code is:
```
        cmd = 'username %s' % name
        if value is not None:
            cmd += ' role %s' % value
        else:
            cmd += ' no role'
        return self.configure(cmd)
```

This works:
```
        cmd = 'username %s' % name
        if value is not None:
            cmd += ' role %s' % value
        else:
            cmd = 'default username %s role' % name
        return self.configure(cmd)
```

issue_title: Feature request: a special "<text>" value for the hooks.yaml "files" field
issue_body:
Hi,

I had to implement this kind of "binary-files-filtering-out" today:
https://github.com/Lucas-C/pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/pre_commit_hooks/utils.py#L10

So I made a quick overview of the existing checkers, and I found only 2 that are targeting **any** text file: [end-of-file-fixer](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/hooks.yaml#L76) and [trailing-whitespaces](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/hooks.yaml#L106). Both use a long list of known text files suffixes.

If ever you think this use case could come in handy, I'd suggest to provide a magic value like `<any text file>` to the `files` parameter in _hooks.yaml_, so that hooks developers don't have to re-implement this filtering each time.

You can freely re-use the code I mentioned above if it comes handy to you.

Also, sorry for the 3-issues-opened-in-a-row :) I got really excited by this tool and ideas for improvements just flowed in ! But they are really just humble features suggestions.

Cheers

```
        if 'm' in params or not hasattr(self, "means_"): # <= oops
```

issue_title: Memory leak
issue_body:
To quickly reproduce the problem, you need to reduce the value of the argument `send_inteval` to a very small value and see what happens with the memory.

Here is an example of code for reproduce a memory leak:

```python
import gc
import asyncio
import aiozipkin as az
from functools import partial
from pympler import muppy, summary


def mem_test(loop):
    gc.collect()
    try:
        all_objects = muppy.get_objects()
        sum = summary.summarize(all_objects)
        summary.print_(sum)
    finally:
        loop.call_later(1, partial(mem_test, loop))


async def run():
    zipkin_address = 'http://127.0.0.1:9411'
    endpoint = az.create_endpoint('minimal_example', ipv4='127.0.0.1')

    tracer = az.create(zipkin_address, endpoint, sample_rate=1.0,
                       send_inteval=0.001)

    await asyncio.sleep(60)

    await tracer.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    loop.call_soon(partial(mem_test, loop))
    try:
        loop.run_until_complete(run())
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        pass
```

The problem in function `_wait` of `Transport`.

It's difficult for me to explain this behavior, but replacing the function `asyncio.as_completed` to `asyncio.wait` solves this problem

```python
    async def _wait(self) -> None:
        self._timer = asyncio.ensure_future(
            asyncio.sleep(self._send_interval, loop=self._loop),
            loop=self._loop)

        await asyncio.wait([self._timer, self._ender], loop=self._loop,
                           return_when=asyncio.FIRST_COMPLETED)
```

issue_title: Requirejs config and path name
issue_body:
The requirejs path shortcuts for things like `jquery-ui` defined in `page.html` don't match the actual names of the packages. For example, `jqueryui` should be `jquery-ui` etc...

It could help transitioning to the nodejs world to use the actual package names instead.

See e.g. the shim by @jdfreder here: https://github.com/ipython/ipywidgets/blob/master/ipywidgets/static/widgets/js/requirejs-shim.js#L25

@minrk @jdfreder

issue_body:
Conference season abstracts are due soon (GCC and ISMB/BOSC). I (@jmchilton) would like to follow up last year's talk "Planemo – A Galaxy Tool SDK" with a talk "Planemo – A Scientific Workflow SDK" suitable for both BOSC and GCC.

Talk Outline:
- [ ] Overview of Recent Galaxy Workflow Enhancements:
  - [ ] Implicit connections.
    - [X] ~~Backend https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/commit/e0a5e82bdae407535b9d7c98e3dcf851b63d01a0~~
    - [ ] UI (TODO)
    - [ ] Slide
  - [ ] Nested Workflows.
    - [X] ~~First pass in 16.01. https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/1306~~
    - [ ] Follow up - allow "upgrading" workflows.
    - [ ] Slides
  - [ ] Labels on all the things.
    - [x] ~~Implementation https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/1306~~
    - [ ] Slide
  - [ ] Collection Operations and Patterns.
    - [ ] Implementation (Issue https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/1644, Failed PR https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/1313) New PR (https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/2434)
    - [ ] Slides
  - [ ] UI enhancements.
    - [ ] Slide
- [ ] Format 2 Galaxy Workflows - A writable specification for native Galaxy workflows.
  - [x] Implement population of default tool state (TODO: create Galaxy issue and PR)
  - [X] Better handling of conditionals (https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/1777)
  - [ ] Describe format, give examples.
    - [ ] Slides
  - [X] Implement a library on top of bioblend. https://github.com/jmchilton/gxformat2
  - [ ] Usage.
    - [ ] Serving Workflows
      - [X] Profiles for workflow development. #445.
      - [X] Serve for Galaxy workflows (`planemo serve <workflow>`). https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/pull/454
      - [x] Enhanced profiles for Galaxy. https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/447.
      - [ ] Slides
    - [ ] Running Workflows (`planemo run <workflow> <job>`)
      - [x] Initial engine implementation https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/pull/454
      - [x] Refine engine for workflows
      - [ ] Slides
    - [ ] Testing Workflows (`planemo test <workflow>`)
      - [ ] Pull Galaxy test stuff out into galaxy-lib for reuse here.
      - [x] Refine engine for testing.
      - [ ] Slides
- [ ] ~~The State of the CWL (skip at BOSC)~~ (@mr-c submitted an abstract - will cover this).
  - [ ] ~~Slides (Michael and Peter will probably have something to use there).~~
- [x] Building and Testing CWL Tools with Planemo
  - [X] Build and lint. (https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/pull/466)
  - [x] Test with cwltool.
  - [x] Test with Galaxy.
  - [x] Serve in Galaxy.
- [ ] Building and Testing CWL Workflows with Planemo
  - [ ] Lint and test with cwltool.
  - [ ] Future plans - Galaxy support.
- [ ] Planemo Machine - An Environment for Galaxy and CWL workflow development.

Too Stretched Goals:
- [ ] Workflows from file paths like tools (https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/1740)

Remaining dev priorities:
- [ ] Emphemaris and shed picture for gxformat2 workflows!
- [ ] Implement `--slurm`.

issue_title: Poor contrast in HTML file between success/failure in overview
issue_body:
I'm red-green colour blind, and I cannot tell any difference between the success and failure messages in the left hand overview. `:P`

It would be useful to do some combination of the following:

- increase colour intensity
- visually distinguish pass/fail (e.g. bold for failures)
- textually indicate pass/fail for those with poorer vision than me or using screen readers (e.g. "Pass" or "Fail" in the `(Test #1)` sections)

Galaxy is pretty horrifically disability unfriendly, but since planemo produces easily modified HTML reports, it'd be nice if they were a bit more accommodating.

issue_title: Partition Disk
issue_body:
Hey,

Is there a way to detect if an image has a partitiontable? We have different images where some contain a partitiontable and some are partition images (ie image start with NTFS header). 

```python
volume_path_spec = path_spec_factory.Factory.NewPathSpec(
    definitions.TYPE_INDICATOR_TSK_PARTITION, location=u'/',
    parent=path_spec)

    volume_system = tsk_volume_system(TSKVolumeSystem()
    volume_system.Open(volume_path_spec)
```

Depending on the image is throws an exception "Cannot determine partition type". Changing definitions between TYPE_INDICATOR_TSK and TYPE_INDICATOR_TSK_PARTITION seems to be working, but I couldn't find a way to automatically derive that information from the other objects

```python
os_path_spec = factory.Factory.NewPathSpec(
    definitions.TYPE_INDICATOR_OS, location=image_location)

type_indicator = analyzer.Analyzer.GetStorageMediaImageTypeIndicators(
    os_path_spec)

path_spec = factory.Factory.NewPathSpec(
    type_indicator[0], parent=os_path_spec)
```

I noticed that plaso can work with both type of images, I couldn't find where this detection is implemented :(

issue_title: Research Subnet.discovery
issue_body:
The UI allows you to associate a subnet with a "Discovery Proxy". Here's a screenshot demonstrating the capability:

![screenshot_2015-05-15_12-57-20](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1012989/7657767/8b6ebfa8-fb02-11e4-96f9-0ab64e5f741c.png)

When you hit the "submit" button, a pseudo HTTP POST request is submitted to `https://<hostname>/subnet/<subnet-id>-<subnet-name>`. Here's a subset of the payload:

```json
{
    "_method": "put",
    "subnet": {"discovery_id": 1},
}
```

"discovery" appears to be a property of the Subnet entity. Can we add this proeprty to the entity? Unfortunately, no. There's a couple big problems:

* HTTP GET responses don't include a "discovery" or "discovery_id" attribute.
* HTTP PUT calls appear to ignore any "discovery_id" attribute that is submitted.
* HTTP PUT responses don't include a "discovery_id" attribute.

What to do? I'm not sure.

issue_title: Useful against pollution of interpreter state
issue_body:
Just wanted to point that this package can be a useful protection against "pollution" of the interpreter state.

For example when testing behaviour of custom imports, given the global state of `sys`, and the cache mechanisms, having boxed processes prevent unknowingly reusing a module imported from another test.
And there might be more cases like this one.

We should probably gather them in one place (`README` ?) as they might be a bit esoteric for the newcomer, and it would be useful to pin them down.

issue_title: support for transactions
issue_body:
The REST API has a transaction feature that allows you to chunk together multiple operations and commit them atomically.

This feature is very useful for operations that touch many different parts of the BIG-IP, but if they were committed separately, might leave the BIG-IP in an inconsistent state.

**Steps To Reproduce**

(I followed this: https://github.com/confluentinc/cp-docker-images/wiki/Getting-Started)

(I also tested against our production kafka and saw the same segfault)

2. run following code:
```python
from confluent_kafka import Producer, Consumer, KafkaError, KafkaException

KAFKA_SERVER = 'localhost:29092'
KAFKA_TOPIC = 'test.topic'
CONSUMER_GROUP = 'test-group'

CONSUMER_CONFIG = {'bootstrap.servers': KAFKA_SERVER,
                   'enable.auto.commit': False,
                   'group.id': CONSUMER_GROUP,
                   'default.topic.config': {'auto.offset.reset': 'earliest'}}


def sendKafkaMessage(topic, message):
    producer = Producer({'bootstrap.servers': KAFKA_SERVER})
    producer.produce(topic, message)
    producer.flush()


def getKafkaMessages(consumer):
    messages = []

    consumer.subscribe([KAFKA_TOPIC])
    try:
        while True:
            message = consumer.poll(timeout=1.0)
            if message is None:
                continue
            error = message.error()
            if error:
                if error.code() == KafkaError._PARTITION_EOF:
                    break
                else:
                    raise KafkaException(error)
            else:
                messages.append(message.value())
                consumer.commit(async=False)
    finally:
        consumer.close()

    return messages


if __name__ == '__main__':
    consumer = Consumer(**CONSUMER_CONFIG)

    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message1')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message2')

    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message10')
    print getKafkaMessages(consumer)
```

This will segfault during the second `getKafkaMessages` call, specifically when `consumer.subscribe` runs again. I'm expecting that the consumer would be able to subscribe again and pick up where it left off via consumer group and commit.

Is this a bug or user error?

Thanks for your time!
Jack

issue_title: Producer in child process not sending messages
issue_body:
I was looking into using python's multiprocessing library to handle producing messages from a child process. However, it seems that messages are not being sent when produced from a child process. My (simplified) setup:

```python
import multiprocessing

from confluent_kafka import Producer


class MyProducer(multiprocessing.Process):
    def __init__(self, kafka_bootstrap: str, kafka_topic: str, queue: multiprocessing.JoinableQueue):
        multiprocessing.Process.__init__(self)
        self.daemon = True
        self.kafka_topic = kafka_topic
        conf = {
            'bootstrap.servers': kafka_bootstrap,
            'api.version.request': True
        }
        self.producer = Producer(**conf)
        self.queue = queue

    def run(self):
        while True:
            msg = self.queue.get()
            if msg is None:
                print('Shutting down child process...')
                break
            self.producer.produce(self.kafka_topic, msg)
            print('flushing')
            self.producer.flush()  # Added in temporarily to show this issue
            print('flushed')  # Never prints
            self.queue.task_done()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    msg_queue = multiprocessing.JoinableQueue()
    child = MyProducer('localhost:9092', 'mytopic', msg_queue)
    child.start()

    conf = {
        'bootstrap.servers': 'localhost:9092',
        'api.version.request': True
    }
    producer = Producer(**conf)

    messages = ['M1', 'M2', 'M3', None]
    for m in messages:
        msg_queue.put(m)

    msg_queue.join()
    producer.flush()
```

The above prints the output `flushing`, but never prints `flushed`.

If, instead of forking off a child process, I take the logic of `MyProducer.run()` and execute it in the main process instead, the script works as expected. Any insight into what's happening here?

issue_title: Detection of package version
issue_body:
Four very different version numbers can be read. example: 

    git describe --tags        # 0.6.2.3-1-g78cfc73  (ok)
    python setup.py --version  # 0.5-30-g3a47a5b  (because I forgot to delete the file VERSION)
    pip show saleforce         # or pip freeze (invalid because not used "python setup.py develop")
    git describe               # v0.1.3-615-g78cfc73  (the last annotaded tag 615 commits ago)


1) The cached file `VERSION` is problematic becase it is not under version control and must be deleted regularly. It is so precise number that it is always unvalid under development process.

2) The second number is important because it can be reported incorrectly by the new `__version__` attribute unrelated to the source code.

3) The third problem need not be solved now. Maybe it can be fixed in the next releases by using `git tag -a  the_tag`. The option `-a` is intended for annotated tags, i.e. public releases, while normal tags are for private purposes. If you create tag releases by Github now, it should be good enough.

Discussion:
If I remember, such complicated scripts like "get_tagged_version" where the version is derived from a tag were important for Mercurial, due to chicken - egg problem, because [it is impossible in Mercurial to simultaneously commit and add a tag for the changeset being committed](https://www.mercurial-scm.org/wiki/Tag#How_do_tags_work_in_Mercurial.3F). Therefore the only exact solution was possible without writing the tag into the source code. This is easy with Git (and many other VCS) and I don't see a reason more. You can use a script (or a paper list) at your side to not forget anything, but a static version number should be present without dependency on *.dist-info, *.egg-info and similar info. If the user installs django-salesforce by `-e git+https://...` option,  she can not reliably report the installed version after running git pull.

Possible solutions:

Static version number in the code.

1a) The dependency between source code and git can be **[reversed](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/6786555/automatic-version-number-both-in-setup-py-setuptools-and-source-code)**.

    git tag -a v$(python setup.py --version)

1b) Releases can be created manually by Github.
It is useful that a pre-release version can be simply marked by writing a new `__version__ = ...-dev` to the code, without adding a tag too soon to invalid code. Finally to remove "-dev" and release a tag. Also unofficial micro-numbers can be useful.

2) The version number for `setup.py` can be read by parsing `__init__.py` if the git repository is not present. The current long version number is very good for an installation from git.



(I will continue.)

issue_title: Error reading protobuf spec. validator error: The .mlmodel supplied is of version 3, intended for a newer version of Xcode. This version of Xcode supports model version 2 or earlier.
issue_body:
Ported from https://github.com/apple/turicreate/issues/701

On Linux, loading a spec into an `MLModel` with any specification version works, because it doesn't attempt to validate/compile the model, since it assumes Core ML doesn't exist on the system anyway.

On latest macOS with latest Xcode, loading a spec into an `MLModel` with any supported specification version works, because model validation/compilation succeeds.

However, loading a spec into an `MLModel` with specification version 3 on macOS 10.13 with Xcode 9.4 fails with the error:

```
Error reading protobuf spec. validator error: The .mlmodel supplied is of version 3, intended for a newer version of Xcode. This version of Xcode supports model version 2 or earlier.
```

Expected:

This should work on older macOS/Xcode the same way it works on Linux, perhaps with a runtime warning that predict won't be available.

Actual:

Since this throws a Python exception, it interrupts execution of the caller and in the case of Turi Create, results in an unusable exported model/spec.

issue_title: Berry fails to fetch credentials sporadically
issue_body:
Quotation: 

> We have two assumption that contradict:
Directly after server start, the download of the client and user json succeeds but has corrupt content (probably just empty files) which would explain why the script assume the file is there and python will probably just have a null object and thus returns empty credentials which then are indeed wrong.
IAM policies are only applied eventual consistent just like tags. The server starts but only a minute after that is the IAM policy for the instance profile applied and the app can download the credentials.
One assumption contradicts the other but I don't know which one happens (or if one at all). What we saw is that everything just works if you log in manually afterwards. I think in both cases, increasing the retry counter would help (retry at least 20 times instead of only 3 times or similar).

So the solution would be to raise the retries of fetching oauth credentials.

issue_title: Check-sat with assumptions on non-linear real arithmetic
issue_body:
I noticed differences in how z3 behaves when using the `(check-sat)` and `(check-sat <assumptions>)` commands on non-linear real arithmetic problems.

The latter returns `unknown` on most satisfiable problems when the former is able to answer just fine (see example below).

While I could use push/pop to get the same features as check-sat with assumptions, my understanding is that this impacts performance significantly which was confirmed by my experiments (on linear arithmetic).

Is this a bug, a consequence of the way check-sat with assumptions is implemented, a problem I could avoid with a clever option, or something else?

    ;; Using the "unstable" branch.
    (declare-fun x () Real)
    (declare-fun y () Real)
    (declare-fun act_1 () Bool)
    (declare-fun act_2 () Bool)
    
    ;; This is fine.
    ( assert (=> act_1 (= (* x y) 0.0)) )
    (check-sat act_1)
    ;; sat
    
    ;; This is also fine with a regular check-sat...
    ( assert (= (* x y) 42.0) )
    (check-sat)
    ;; sat
    
    ;; ...but not with check-sat with assumptions.
    ( assert (=> act_2 (= (* x y) 42.0)) )
    (check-sat act_2)
    ;; unknown

issue_title: Build issue: PyWavelets does not install from sdist
issue_body:
When used as a dependency in another Python package (scikit-image), PyWavelets fails to install from sdist with standard Python tooling because it relies on presence of *numpy* in its *setup.py*. Thus, running `pip install --no-binary :all: ...` or `python setup.py sdist` fails with a clean Python installation (no external packages). Since the package is published on PyPI (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/PyWavelets), it needs to be able to build from source distribution (*tar.gz* file) self-sufficiently.

The fact that the package imports *numpy* in *setup.py* indicates that the package needs *numpy* in `setup_requires`. This creates a chicken-and-egg kind of problem that is not easy to hack around, but it is possible. Please take a look at *scipy* and what they are doing in *setup.py*: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/master/setup.py. This may help with fixing this issue for PyWavelets.

issue_title: Enhance otPlatRadioTransmitDone() callback by passing up the ACK frame
issue_body:
This is a proposal to enhance otPlatRadioTransmitDone() callback by **passing up the ACK frame** as part of it.

Current otPlatRadioTransmitDone() callback only pass up the ACK's `aFramPending` information:
```
extern void otPlatRadioTransmitDone(otInstance *aInstance, RadioPacket *aPacket, bool aFramePending,
                                    ThreadError aError);
```
The new otPlatRadioTransmitDone() callback will pass up the whole ACK frame instead:
```
extern void otPlatRadioTransmitDone(otInstance *aInstance, RadioPacket *aPacket, RadioPacket *aAckPacket,
                                    ThreadError aError);
```

The benefits of the enhancement:
- The ACK messages could also be used for link quality evaluation (short term);
- To support the Enhanced ACK which may be used in some new features (long term).

Please give comments if you have any suggestions or concerns, thanks for that.

issue_title: COMM_KA vrs RELAY_{tx|rx} spec issue/question
issue_body:
Hmm - There seems to be a large timing issue that manifests in multiple ways, no one way is clearly the problem.  This could also be due to the way the Android Commissioning app is structured :-(

For the purposes of this discussion, I want to divide the commissioning process into 3 phases.

Phase 1 - is prior to the DISCOVERY REQUEST
Phase 2 - is during the time the RLY_{TX|RX} is active
Phase 3 - is after JOIN_FIN

During Phase 1 - the COMM_KA(keep alive) is a very useful message to determine if the application should shut down, or give up - etc - this make sense.

During Phase 2 - (when RLY_{TX|RX} is active) the COMM_KA(keepalive) becomes very problematic, and often does not occur at an actual rate that it needs to.

This also has to do with TIMEOUT_COMM_PET (50secs) and TIMEOUT_LEAD_PET(50secs), THR_DTLS_INIT_RETRANSMIT_TIMEOUT(8sec start), note the RE-TRANSMIT timeout increases exponentially.

In the android app (current design), there is a thread that does the keep alive process. This thread does not actually send the COMM_KA over the air (wifi), instead it queues the request into a work queue, another thread takes the COMM_KA  {and the RLY_TXRX messages} and sends these via the DTLS session to the AGENT.

The actual transmission time of the COMM_KA can be upwards of 20 seconds after the request is queued. Why so long? Because the under powered joiner the DTLS session with the JOINER is takes more time, in addition if there are *ANY* over the air re-transmissions of RF packets, it takes longer for the *LARGE* packets to be reassembled. 

In effect, the COMM_KA and RELAY_{TX|RX} are serialized and msg(A) slows down msg(B) because the messages are processed in order.  This often brings us just past the TIMEOUTs,  in any of the 4 parties involved (AndroidApp,   NCP,  JOINING_ROUTER,  and JOINING_DEVICE) it is not consistent, and not reliable.

One PROPOSED change - is to treat the RELAY_{TX|RX} activity as an alternate COMM_KA, the rational is: If the RELAY_TX & RX is occurring, this is occurring with the Border Agent, and is being sent by the COMMISSIONER - if those messages are succeeding (making forward progress in any form) then the commissioner has not walked away.

issue_title: passing array=pyexcel.Sheet to pyexcel.save_as is an un-intended use
issue_body:
Hi all,

I just realize that passing array=pyexcel.Sheet is an un-intended use. And these code changes are against the interface between pyexcel-io and pyexcel is a dictionary of two dimensional array. I am sorry that this interface is not documented publicly. The reason for such an interface is to break the tight coupling between pyexcel and pyexcel-io.

https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-text/blob/master/pyexcel_text/__init__.py#L125
https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-text/blob/master/pyexcel_text/__init__.py#L185

I guess this line has played  a mis-leading role in it:

https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-text/blob/master/tests/test_io.py#L208

With that said, pyexcel-text is an exception among the pyexcel-io plugins because it also serves as a presentation plugin to pyexcel, in other words, pyexcel-text is a tightly coupled with pyexcel.

So, I will need to find a way to in-corporate the new use cases, either making this un-intended use as intended or implementing them within the interface set out.

issue_title: csvjson writing GeoJSON fails when type is not Point
issue_body:
csvjoin fails if the `--lon` or `--lat` columns are empty, which is what `in2csv -f geojson` emits for non-Point

```
$ wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lyzidiamond/learn-geojson/master/geojson/pdxplaces.geojson
$ in2csv -f geojson pdxplaces.geojson > pdxplaces.csv
$ head -1  pdxplaces.csv
id,Name,Contributor,Reason,my polygon,Utility,Coffee,geojson,type,longitude,latitude
$ wc -l pdxplaces.csv
18 pdxplaces.csv
$ csvcut -c type,longitude,latitude pdxplaces.csv | csvgrep -c type -m Polygon | csvlook
| type    | longitude | latitude |
| ------- | --------- | -------- |
| Polygon |           |          |
| Polygon |           |          |
| Polygon |           |          |
$ grep Polygon pdxplaces.csv
[manually edited output for brevity]
...,Polygon,,
...,Polygon,,
...,Polygon,,
$ csvjson --lon longitude --lat latitude --indent=4 pdxplaces.csv > pdxplaces.csv.geojson
float() argument must be a string or a number, not 'NoneType'
```

Occurs in 1.0.2 and master.

It works when the Polygon's are removed
```
$ grep -v Polygon pdxplaces.csv > pdxplaces-points.csv
$ wc -l pdxplaces-points.csv 
15 pdxplaces-points.csv
$ csvjson --lon longitude --lat latitude --indent=4 pdxplaces-points.csv > pdxplaces.csv.geojson
```

issue_title: Date must be preserved as text
issue_body:
I am using *csvsql 1.0.2* with something as
```sh
cat myDataset.csv  | \
csvsql --insert --tables t --db  postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/sandbox   
```
where `myDataset.csv` have column `c1` with non-ISO dates, that  must be interpreted as strings,
```
c1,c2
03/02/2018 10:21:42,etc.
03/02/2018 18:03:52,etc.
05/02/2018 20:11:20,etc.
```
 but  `csvsql`  enforced  c01 to be interpreted as *timestamp without time zone*. A big mistake, generating future dates,
```
select c1 from quest1resps ;
    c1    
---------------------
 2018-03-02 10:21:42
 2018-03-02 18:03:52
 2018-05-02 20:11:20
 ```
It is not reliable. Any "magic" interpretation must be added only when an option is flagged: **default behaviour must be the most reliable**, not the "most magic".

issue_title: Allow `-H` to relabel headers
issue_body:
Consider data sources that generate CSV without headers -- this might be due to chunking or something similar in a processing pipeline.

With `-H` as it stands we can use this data but the resulting query code is confusing. If `-H` or a related argument accepted a comma separated list of new column names, it would alleviate the confusion thus resulting.

A penny for your thoughts.

issue_title: csvsql without tty always tries to read stdin
issue_body:
The following snip works from a terminal but fails in a non-interactive session (we hit it in Jenkins, but I'd guess it also fails in cron)

```
csvsql --table foo --query "select * from foo" foo.csv
``` 

You get a `StopIteration` exception because csvsql is trying to read from stdin, which has nothing coming (this line: https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit/blob/205175fb70745b80db19acd4c314ad6c774b7fc0/csvkit/utilities/csvsql.py#L57). There's a previous discussion of the issue at https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit/issues/342 and https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit/issues/627, but the linked commit doesn't solve the issue. 

We're working around it by either always sending something in to stdin when running from a job. 

I think csvsql should require naming stdin with a "-" when you want to read from both files named as arguments and stdin. This is how `cat` works:

```
echo "foo" | cat /tmp/file.csv  # just prints file.csv
echo "foo" | cat - /tmp/file.csv  # prints foo, then file.csv
echo "foo" | cat /tmp/file.csv -  # prints file.csv, then foo
```

issue_title: Fail to validate HTTPS server while using custom CA file
issue_body:
Hello httplib2.
I am using httplib2==0.9.2.

My use case is quite simple: we have a private network and have setup some HTTPS services. These services provide certificates signed by a site-local CA authority. All certificates are signed using SHA-256 as signing algorithm.

Take that: 
  * a self-signed root CA certificate exists inside `/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt`  (the standard debian location)
  * the certificate for CN `foo.localdomain` is signed by this local authority, and is installed to a web server https://foo.localdomain

I'am trying to use httplib2 to communicate with this service: 
```python
h = httplib2.Http(ca_certs='/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt')
resp, cont = h.request('https://foo.localdomain')
```

issue_title: unexpected behaviour of sampling in Pipeline
issue_body:
I have been trying to integrate the `imblearn` samplers into a pipeline. However, there is some behaviour that I do not understand. The code below is inspired by the documentation.

```python
from collections import Counter
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
from sklearn.cross_validation import train_test_split as tts
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier as KNN
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
from imblearn.under_sampling import RandomUnderSampler
from imblearn.pipeline import Pipeline

X, y = make_classification(
    n_classes=2, class_sep=2, weights=[0.1, 0.9],
    n_informative=3, n_redundant=1, flip_y=0,
    n_features=20, n_clusters_per_class=1,
    n_samples=1000, random_state=10
)

print('Original dataset shape {}'.format(Counter(y)))

# split the dataset
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = tts(X, y, random_state=42)

# define estimators
rus = RandomUnderSampler(random_state=42)
knn = KNN()
```

I then use these elements to build a pipeline...

```python
# build pipeline
pipeline = Pipeline([('rus', rus), ('knn', knn)])

# fit pipline and make prediction
pipeline.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_hat = pipeline.predict(X_test)
print(classification_report(y_test, y_hat))
```

...which gives:

```
             precision    recall  f1-score   support

          0       0.80      1.00      0.89        24
          1       1.00      0.97      0.99       226

avg / total       0.98      0.98      0.98       250

```

Then I repeat same operation sequentially outside a pipeline...

```python
# sample training and test sets
X_train_sampled, y_train_sampled = rus.fit_sample(X_train, y_train)
X_test_sampled, y_test_sampled = rus.fit_sample(X_test, y_test)

# fit knn with sampled data and make prediction
knn.fit(X_train_sampled, y_train_sampled)
y_hat = knn.predict(X_test_sampled)
print(classification_report(y_test_sampled, y_hat))
```

...which gives:

```
             precision    recall  f1-score   support

          0       0.96      1.00      0.98        24
          1       1.00      0.96      0.98        24

avg / total       0.98      0.98      0.98        48

```

They lead to different outputs, with the latter being the one I would have expected. Specifically, it seems like the pipeline does not undersample (see support column of the `classification_report`). What am I doing wrong?

Thanks for the development!

issue_body:
This should've been obvious from the beginning (given offlineimap's development), but I guess it's never too late: We should switch to sqlite for storing the status metadata in `<statusfolder>/<pair>/<collection>.items`.

`.collections` files should still be JSON, only the format for `.items` changes. At least I see no reason to do otherwise.
* The codebase for loading and saving `.collections` and `.items` files is weirdly entangled. We need to fix that.

Goal: Ability to modify status in-place (instead of having "load" and "save" steps) opens the door to optimizing memory-efficiency of sync. I played with this once and it's not easy. We'll see.
